items.title: Vindicator Low Intensity Power 32.5k/36k
items.cleaned_transcript:
For the three-button build, you just want to autoattack. That is pretty much it. Your three buttons are Impossible Odds, Energy Meld, and your dodge key. You want to minimize how many times you dodge, but make sure to keep up Forerunner of Death. Every time you dodge, you get that buff, which makes you do 25% more damage. To maximize your damage, minimize how many times you dodge while still keeping up Forerunner of Death.

Make sure your endurance does not go to full, because you get a damage buff whenever your endurance is not full. Use Energy Meld to make sure you have enough energy to keep up Impossible Odds. Otherwise, you just autoattack, and that is really it.

For the four-button build, your opener is two dodge rolls into activating Impossible Odds, into Energy Meld, into skill 2, and then you autoattack. More or less, you want to time skill 2 followed by your dodge key. That is your primary combo. Anytime Energy Meld comes up, because skill 2 has a five-second cooldown and Energy Meld has a 20-second cooldown, your cooldowns will line up. Anytime Energy Meld comes up, use Energy Meld, skill 2, and dodge roll. Those are your two main combos. Keep doing that and autoattacking. Try to finish your autoattack chains.

This build is a little different. If you look at the build, you will see that I am running out of energy. Most fights in the game are not going to have you autoattacking for a very long time like you do on the golem. There will be phases, you will be running around, hitting adds, or doing something else. You have two primary options for recovering your energy if you run out.

The first option is for phases. For example, if you are playing this on Vale Guardian or Gorseval, or you are running around or waiting for something to happen, just turn off Impossible Odds. In combat, your energy will build up to 100. When you get back to smacking the opponent, reactivate Impossible Odds and continue playing the build as normal. If you want, you can also use skill 4 and skill 5 to dump some of the extra energy you gained while waiting for the phase.

The second option is to swap to your other legend. If you are in an extended fight, have used up all your energy in Shiro, and notice you are nearly at 0% and cannot cast more skill 2s, swap to Jalis, activate Vengeful Hammers, and continue spamming skill 2 and your dodge key. I recommend waiting on Energy Meld until you are back in Shiro, so you can use Energy Meld to gain back a little more energy and camp Shiro again. Those are your two options for energy gain: deactivate Impossible Odds during phases, or activate Jalis to regain energy in an extended encounter. It will be a small DPS loss, but not a big one. For the most part, you are trying to camp Shiro.

For the overall build, skill 2 is your big damage skill. You pretty much never want to press skill 3, and it is also a movement-lock skill, so try not to press skill 3. Skill 4 and skill 5 are worth pressing if you want to dump a bunch of extra energy. Energy Meld is how you are able to stay on Shiro without legend swapping. Every time you press Energy Meld, you regain 25 energy. If you are playing either build, make sure you do not run out of energy. With the three-button build, your energy expenditure is fairly low, so you want to minimize how many times you cast it. With the four-button build, your energy expenditure is fairly high, so you want to maximize how many times you cast it. Basically, cast it and make sure you do not run out of energy.

Trait-wise, nothing too crazy is going on. The whole top line is damage. The middle line has Rising Tide, which is more damage while you are above the health threshold. It is mostly more damage. In Invocation, the middle trait can be swapped if you want. I take Spirit Boon because you gain a little bit of stability if you swap to Jalis. If you time your stuff correctly and swap to Jalis, you can gain a little stability, which might be nice. You can also take Rapid Flow if you want extra sustain, and there is also Incensed Response. Anything in that middle Invocation slot is fine. Take whatever you want or need. Spirit Boon is more selfless, while the others are more selfish.

You never want to swap the last Invocation trait, Roiling Mists. You gain a lot of extra crit chance, which is good, and it also converts a portion of your outgoing critical strike damage into healing. You gain a decent amount of natural sustain while playing the build.

In Vindicator, you get a lot more damage from Leviathan Strength. You want to make sure you never reach full endurance. If you play the build how I recommended, at least with the four-button version, you press your dodge key after every skill 2. With the three-button version, just make sure you never reach full endurance before pressing the dodge key.

We take the middle trait, Angsiyan's Trust. That is how we are able to camp Shiro without legend swapping. Energy Meld gives you energy. The other traits are mostly more power and more damage above a health threshold. Death Drop is how you deal a lot of damage. It is a 25% damage increase for 10 seconds after you dodge. Try to dodge relatively often, but not too frequently. Do not dump all of your dodge rolls right away and then sit there without vigor, unable to dodge in time. For the most part, you should be fine. Just do not forget to dodge.

Utility-wise, nothing too crazy is going on. You are camping Shiro and using Impossible Odds. You have a few ways to CC. Your elite, Jade Winds, CCs, but you need the energy to use it. If you are running low on energy and there is a break bar, swap to Jalis. I would recommend running off-hand staff and using staff skill 5 to CC. My recommendation for CC is either use your Shiro elite as option one, or run off-hand staff, swap to Jalis, activate Vengeful Hammers, and use staff skill 5 to CC.

To show what I mean for the four-button build and how you can regain energy, imagine you are in combat, fighting, using your skills, autoattacking, using skill 2, and running out of energy. If you are phasing, turn off Impossible Odds. Your energy gain is pretty good, and you will start going above 50%. While you are in combat, it will keep rising until 100%. If you are dealing with adds, waiting for a phase, or running around, and then you are ready to come back, you can dodge roll, use skill 5 or skill 4, dump the extra energy, and restart from there.

The other option is that if you are running out of energy, swap to Jalis, activate Vengeful Hammers, and keep playing like normal. Try to save Energy Meld. Once you swap back to Shiro, use Impossible Odds, Energy Meld, dodge, and keep playing as normal. Use Jalis for energy gain, or turn off Impossible Odds for energy gain.

If you are interested in playing this build as alacrity, you need to slot the last Vindicator trait instead of the first trait: Saint of zu Heltzer. This makes your dodge roll give barrier, healing, and alacrity. You do not need to make many changes to how you play the build. For the most part, the rule stays the same. If you are playing the three-button build, dodge roll to avoid being at full endurance. If you are playing the four-button build, dodge roll after skill 2.

The one thing I will say is that this makes your dodge roll stop doing damage. You may need to mess around a little with your opener, because the opener starts with dodge rolls. In most encounters, once the encounter starts, you will enter combat naturally. If you are practicing it, keep in mind that Energy Meld only gives a decent amount of energy when it is used in combat. Your opener for the alacrity build will mostly be two dodge rolls, because you want to get a decent amount of alacrity right away, and then start autoattacking and doing what you want to do.

The second change is your gear. My recommendation is 40% to 60% boon duration. Around 50% will be fairly strong, 40% is more on the min-max side, and 60% is safer. I will leave a 50% recommendation in the description. If you want to build it yourself with whatever you have, aim for 40% to 60% boon duration while keeping your crit chance at 100%. My other strong recommendation is Fireworks rune, because it gives about 25% boon duration. That is something you can slot in if you are trying to get to 40% to 60%.

Again, I will leave my recommended set in the description, but if you want to build it yourself, aim for 40% to 60% boon duration while keeping yourself at 100% crit chance. Diviner's is the power offensive support stat. Compared with berserker's, you lose power, precision, and ferocity, and trade that for concentration. Concentration is boon duration.

That is it for the alacrity showcase. You are able to keep up alacrity fairly well. You may want to switch your opener to two dodge rolls so you can give a decent amount of alacrity right away. Otherwise, you play it the same way: either dodge roll once in a while to make sure you are not at full endurance, or dodge roll after pressing skill 2. As long as you have vigor, press Energy Meld, and dodge roll, you should be okay on alacrity uptime. That is pretty much all I have for the build. Thank you for watching, and I hope this build helps you.
